title: Signal Tanker Weekly Report
summary:
Chart of the Week: Crude Oil Flows from AG to China
Crude oil flows from AG to China have been fluctuating at a lower level since the last peak in mid-April
Data Source: https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/tanker-dynamic-oilflows/2p728j/335787498?h=pttO_--n2uquJt_LWFQJYjAsbbnDcan2P6z-RxUh-GA
In the last days of July, the outlook for crude oil freight rates showed signs of weakness as the growth of demand in tonne days continued to decline. Additionally, the daily 25-day moving average of crude oil flows from Arabian Gulf countries to China has been consistently decreasing since its peak in mid-April. During July, the volume of oil flows reached levels even lower than those recorded in the previous year.

children.summary:
Crude oil flows from AG to China have been fluctuating at a lower level since the last peak in mid-April
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-07-28T110003.530.png | assets/2023-07-28_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-07-28t110003530_1e38334b3035.png
Data Source: https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/tanker-dynamic-oilflows/2p728j/335787498?h=pttO_--n2uquJt_LWFQJYjAsbbnDcan2P6z-RxUh-GA
In the last days of July, the outlook for crude oil freight rates showed signs of weakness as the growth of demand in tonne days continued to decline. Additionally, the daily 25-day moving average of crude oil flows from Arabian Gulf countries to China has been consistently decreasing since its peak in mid-April.

children.title: ‘Dirty' WS VLCC - Suezmax - Aframax Weaker
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-07-28T110131.980.png | assets/2023-07-28_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-07-28t110131980_7772b7de90e5.png
The last week of July was quiet, with crude oil tanker freight rates weakening as the VLCC MEG -China route struggles to maintain firmer momentum for the summer season. VLCC MEG-China freight rates fell to 50.5 WS, down 2 points from the previous week, while rates have yet to fall below the 50 mark.

children.title: ‘Product' WS LR Firmer
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-07-28T110242.009.png | assets/2023-07-28_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-07-28t110242009_d705da398326.png
LR2 AG freight rates rose to levels above WS100 this week, which is 20 points higher than two weeks ago, with signs of stronger momentum near the end of the month.

children.title: Panamax Weaker
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-07-28T110709.330.png | assets/2023-07-28_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-07-28t110709330_6b0a9cf3d33f.png
Panamax Carib-to-USG rates fell to WS180, down 15 points from two weeks ago.

children.title: ‘Clean' MR Firmer
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-07-28T120430.662.png | assets/2023-07-28_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-07-28t120430662_e35a63254e5a.png
MR1 rates for the Baltic continent were at 180WS, 25 points higher than the previous week, and it seems that the firmer dynamics towards the end of July are confirmed. MR2 rates for shipments from the continent to the U.S.

children.title: Dirty (#vessels) - Mixed
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-07-28T120553.055.png | assets/2023-07-28_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-07-28t120553055_0e485904c645.png
Crude oil tanker supply showed a mixed picture with a downward trend in the last week of July, with signs of decline in the VLCC and Suezmax segments, while the increase in the Aframax segment seems to be continuing. VLCC Ras Tanura: The current count is about 63 ships, which is 10 less than the average for the year, and there is a tendency for the number to drop further toward the end of the month.

children.title: 'Clean' LR2 (#vessels) - Decreasing
children.summary: Image reference: unnamed - 2023-07-28T120639.193.png | assets/2023-07-28_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-07-28t120639193_e922bc9544db.png

children.title: MR1 (#vessels) - Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-07-28T120710.697.png | assets/2023-07-28_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-07-28t120710697_702be425d5bb.png
Clean LR2 AG Jubail: The current number of ships is currently 10, which is 6 less than the previous week and 3 less than the average for the year. Clean MR1 Algeria Skikda: The current ship count is now down to 27 ships, which is about 10 ships less than the previous week and 7 ships less than the average for the year.

children.title: ‘Dirty' Tonne Days Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-07-28T120818.902.png | assets/2023-07-28_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-07-28t120818902_fe2d5ed16f27.png
Dirty tonne days: In the last week of July, the downward revision of the percentage increase in demand (tonnage days) continues in all size classes of oil tankers. It remains uncertain how demand will evolve in early August, with hopes for Chinese crude oil demand growth lifting today's sentiment.

children.title: ‘Clean' Tonne Days Panamax / MR Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-07-28T120903.137.png | assets/2023-07-28_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-07-28t120903137_39a2e52296f2.png
Panamax tonne days: In the last week of July, the significant downward correction continues, reaching a low not seen since the beginning of last year. Clean MR tonne days: The first upward trend in tonne-miles continued in July for the MR2 size compared to the previous week, after a weak picture in the previous days, while a downward trend was again observed for the MR1 size.
